summary: Remastered sound‑board capture of a legendary 9/20/70 Fillmore East show – pristine acoustic set and smoking electric jams despite lingering tape hiss.
review: The September 20, 1970 Fillmore East performance is hailed as one of the band’s finest from that year: an immaculate acoustic opening (Rosie McFall, New Speedway, Brokedown Palace) followed by a fiery second set highlighted by Easy Wind, To‑Lay‑Me‑Down and an incendiary Not Fade Away > Caution sequence.  The Sir Mick remaster cleans up much of the original tape noise, though some hiss and occasional volume swings remain; overall the recording is clear enough to showcase the band’s tight interplay.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 % of reviews are 5‑star, praising both performance and the remaster; only a handful note hiss or mix quirks, so the combined show (70 %) + recording (30 %) rating lands in the 4.5 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remaster removes most tape hiss and fixes volume dips, but some residual hiss, warble and occasional drum‑mix highs remain; overall clarity is strong with good instrument separation.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away > Caution > Feedback > Goodnight
show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic set opens the show with gentle, blue‑grass‑flavored numbers that build into a high‑energy electric second half; reviewers note the pacing feels natural and the transition from acoustic to electric is seamless.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His lead guitar is described as “out of control” and his piano on To Lay Me Down receives special praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass noted for its warm, woody tone (“wood and strings”) that grounds the acoustic set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) steals the spotlight on Easy Wind with searing lead work; his rhythm/lead duel with Jerry is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ shines on Me and My Uncle and Attics; keyboards are generally praised though some mixes put drums too high.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s drumming is energetic but a few reviewers mention the drum mix being overly loud in spots.
